growth_records.strain: NRC-1 (Delta ura3 parent background; ChIP-seq harvest strains AKS134 empty-vector and KAD128 HpyA-HA)
growth_records.cite: Sakrikar et al. An archaeal histone-like protein regulates gene expression in response to salt stress. Nucleic Acids Research (2021). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkab1175. [PMC8682779]
growth_records.snippet: The 50 mL cultures were grown in 125 ml flasks and their growth was monitored by OD600 until the time for harvesting (exponential phase: 36-50 h, OD ~0.2-0.35, growth rate ~0.059 h-1; stationary phase: ~70-140 h, OD ~1.4-1.7, growth rate ~0
growth_records.notes: Prokaryote (archaeon) growth rate only; no substrate uptake, product secretion, or MFA fluxes reported (regulatory/ChIP-seq study). Reported exponential-phase mu ~0.059 h-1 in CM at 4.2 M NaCl (250 g/L NaCl = ~4.28 M), pH 6.8, 42 C, aerobic batch. Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.059 = 11.75 h. A decelerating stationary-phase rate ~0.032 h-1 (td=21.7 h) was also reported for the same culture but is not a true max growth rate. Separately, WT vs delta-hpyA mu_max were compared at optimal salt (4.2 M NaCl) and reduced salt (3.4 M NaCl): reduced salt slowed WT mu_max to 89% of the optimal-salt value, but absolute mu_max values were given only in Figure 1 (not in text) so are not captured here. Values are approximate (~) as printed. Other three papers in batch (PMC8670527 Arabidopsis root microbiome; PMC8692400 coral areal N2-fixation rates in ug N cm-2 d-1; PMC8692406 seafloor community 13C qSIP excess-atomic-fraction) report no pure-culture prokaryote uptake/secretion/growth-rate data in GEM-usable units and yield no records.

growth_records.cite: Breuert S et al. Regulated Polyploidy in Halophilic Archaea. PLoS ONE (2006). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0000092. [PMC1762399]
growth_records.snippet: The doubling time of H. salinarum in grown aerobically in complex medium at 42 C is about 4 hours. ... H. salinarum was grown by aerobic respiration at 42oC and 30 oC and by arginine fermentation at 42oC. The doubling times were 4 hours, 8
growth_records.notes: Archaeon (prokaryote). Reported doubling time td=4 h (aerobic respiration, complex medium, 42C). growth_rate mu calculated as mu=ln2/td=0.6931/4=0.173 /h. No uptake/secretion/MFA rates reported (ploidy study). Complex medium composition not stated (referenced to refs 31,40). pH and dilution rate not reported (batch).

growth_records.cite: Breuert S et al. Regulated Polyploidy in Halophilic Archaea. PLoS ONE (2006). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0000092. [PMC1762399]
growth_records.snippet: Two different conditions were used that lower the growth rate by about a factor of two, 1) cultures were grown at 30 C instead of 42 C ... In both cases, the doubling time is around 8 hours. ... The doubling times were 4 hours, 8 hours and
growth_records.notes: Archaeon (prokaryote). Reported doubling time td=8 h (aerobic respiration, complex medium, 30C; ~2-fold slower than 42C). growth_rate mu=ln2/td=0.6931/8=0.087 /h (calculated). No uptake/secretion/MFA rates reported. Complex medium composition not stated.

up.method: fermentation substrate (arginine fermentation); rate not quantified

growth_records.cite: Breuert S et al. Regulated Polyploidy in Halophilic Archaea. PLoS ONE (2006). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0000092. [PMC1762399]
growth_records.snippet: they were grown anaerobically by arginine fermentation instead of aerobic respiration. In both cases, the doubling time is around 8 hours. ... H. salinarum was grown by ... arginine fermentation at 42oC. The doubling times were 4 hours, 8 h
growth_records.notes: Archaeon (prokaryote). Anaerobic arginine fermentation, complex medium, 42C; reported td=8 h. growth_rate mu=ln2/td=0.6931/8=0.087 /h (calculated). Arginine is the fermentation substrate but no quantitative uptake rate given (is_experimental:false). No secretion or MFA fluxes reported. Complex medium composition not stated.

growth_records.medium: Chemically-defined synthetic medium (simplest known; ~4 M salt), composition in Table S1

up.method: maximal respiratory rate measured during growth via fiber-optic O2 meter

up.units: n/a (highest uptake rate; numeric only in Fig 2/4)

sec.method: amino acid analyzer; ~95% of arginine carbon secreted as ornithine via Arg-Orn antiporter
inferred from respiration/arginine fermentation (carbamate kinase); not directly quantified

yields.biomass_amino_acid_content: ~503 ug/OD*ml (total AA, ~60% of organic mass, measured); ~19% of consumed carbon incorporated into biomass at late-exponential (aerobic)

growth_records.cite: Gonzalez, Orland et al. Systems Analysis of Bioenergetics and Growth of the Extreme Halophile Halobacterium salinarum. PLoS Computational Biology (2009). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000332. [PMC2674319]
growth_records.snippet: We observed a maximal respiratory rate of approximately 1 micromol O2/OD.ml.hr during growth... aerobic preparations exhibited exponential phase doubling times of only roughly 11 hours, phototrophic cultures demonstrated times close to 50 h
growth_records.notes: Aerobic batch, H. salinarum R1 (DSM 671), 40C, dark, defined amino-acid-rich ~4M-salt medium (Table S1, not in text -> composition_stated=false). growth_rate CALCULATED: mu=ln2/td=0.693/11h=0.0630 /h (td=11h reported). O2 uptake ~1 umol O2/(OD*ml*h) is the reported measured maximal respiratory rate. Individual amino-acid uptake rates are measured (amino acid analyzer time-courses, Figs 2/4) but only shown graphically, so rate=null. Arginine highest uptake, most secreted as ornithine (~95%). Fig 5 fluxome is a model prediction (no numeric fluxes in text). Amino acid biomass content 503 ug/OD*ml measured.

growth_records.medium: Chemically-defined synthetic medium (same as aerobic; ~4 M salt), composition in Table S1

up.units: n/a (rapidly depleted near start; Fig S6)

sec.method: amino acid analyzer; arginine converted to ornithine and secreted despite phototrophy

yields.carbon_incorporation: ~20% of consumed carbon incorporated into biomass at 1.5 OD (14% as measured amino acids); ~80% not incorporated

growth_records.cite: Gonzalez, Orland et al. Systems Analysis of Bioenergetics and Growth of the Extreme Halophile Halobacterium salinarum. PLoS Computational Biology (2009). DOI:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1000332. [PMC2674319]
growth_records.snippet: phototrophic cultures demonstrated times close to 50 hours... When phototrophic cultures reached 1.5 OD, only about 14% of the supplied carbon that had been consumed could be attributed to the amino acid content of the biomass... total carb
growth_records.notes: Phototrophic (anaerobic, light-driven) batch, H. salinarum R1. growth_rate CALCULATED: mu=ln2/td=0.693/50h=0.01386 /h (td~50h reported, ~5-fold slower than aerobic). Same defined medium (composition in Table S1, not in text). Amino-acid uptake/secretion measured (amino acid analyzer, Fig S6) but graphical only -> rate=null. Arginine rapidly depleted and secreted as ornithine even under phototrophy. No O2 uptake (anaerobic). No numeric MFA fluxes in text (Fig 5 fluxome is model prediction).
